apiBaseUrl.title: API Base URL
apiBaseUrl.description: Base URL of the API endpoint (no trailing slash). The lookup value is appended or passed as a parameter based on the Value Position setting. Example: http://ip-api.com/json

valuePosition.title: Lookup Value Position
valuePosition.description: How the lookup value is added to the request. Path: appended to URL (e.g., /json/8.8.8.8). Query: added as query parameter (e.g., ?q=8.8.8.8). Body: sent in POST/PUT request body only.

queryParamName.title: Query Parameter Name
queryParamName.description: Name of the query parameter when Value Position is 'Query'. Example: 'q', 'ip', 'query', 'ipAddress'

extraQueryParams.title: Extra Query Parameters
extraQueryParams.description: Additional query string parameters appended to every request (no leading ? or &). Example: fields=status,country,city,isp,org,query

lookupField.title: Lookup Field
lookupField.description: Event field whose value is sent to the API (e.g., src_ip, ip, hostname, hash)

enrichPrefix.title: Enrichment Field Prefix
enrichPrefix.description: Prefix for all enrichment fields added to events (e.g., geo_ produces geo_country, geo_city)

apiKey.title: API Key / Token
apiKey.description: API key or token. Leave empty for APIs that don't require auth (like ip-api.com free tier).

authHeaderName.title: Custom Auth Header Name
authHeaderName.description: Header name for 'Custom Header' auth type (e.g., X-API-Key, key)

bodyTemplate.title: Request Body Template (POST/PUT)
bodyTemplate.description: JSON body for POST/PUT requests. Use {lookup_value} where the lookup value should go. Leave empty for default {"query": "<value>"}.

selectedFields.title: Selected Response Fields (selective mode)
selectedFields.description: Dot-notation paths to extract from the API response (e.g., score, geo.country, tags)

cacheTTL.title: Cache TTL (seconds)
cacheTTL.description: Cache API responses to reduce duplicate lookups. 0 = no caching.

rateLimitMs.title: Rate Limit (ms between requests)
rateLimitMs.description: Minimum ms between API calls. 0 = no limit.
